title: MXene-Assisted NiFe sulfides for high-performance anion exchange membrane seawater electrolysis

概念_主学科.normalized: 阴离子交换膜海水电解
概念_主学科.evidence: Anion exchange membrane seawater electrolysis is vital for future large-scale green hydrogen production
however enduring a huge challenge that lacks high-stable oxygen evolution reaction electrocatalysts
Herein, we report a robust OER electrocatalyst for AEMSE by integrating MXene (Ti<sub>3</sub>C<sub>2</sub>) with NiFe sulfides
Herein, we report a robust OER electrocatalyst for AEMSE by integrating MXene (Ti<sub>3</sub>C<sub>2</sub>) with NiFe sulfides ((Ni,Fe)S<sub>2</sub>@Ti<sub>3</sub>C<sub>2</sub>)
The strong interaction between (Ni,Fe)S<sub>2</sub> and Ti<sub>3</sub>C<sub>2</sub> induces electron distribution to trigger lattice oxygen mechanism
particularly prohibits the dissolution of Fe species during OER process via the Ti-O-Fe bonding effectively
the good retention of sulfates and the abundant groups of Ti<sub>3</sub>C<sub>2</sub> provide effective Cl<sup>-</sup> resistance
(Ni,Fe)S<sub>2</sub>@Ti<sub>3</sub>C<sub>2</sub> achieves high OER activity (1.598 V@2 A cm<sup>-2</sup>)
and long-term durability (1000 h) in seawater system

概念_主学科.normalized: 工业电流密度 (0.5 A cm-2)
概念_主学科.evidence: Furthermore, AEMSE with industrial current density (0.5 A cm<sup>-2</sup>)
with electrolysis efficiency of 70% and energy consumption of 48.4 kWh kg<sup>-1</sup> H<sub>2</sub>

概念_主学科.term: energy consumption of 48.4 kWh kg<sup>-1</sup> H<sub>2</sub>
概念_主学科.evidence: and energy consumption of 48.4 kWh kg<sup>-1</sup> H<sub>2</sub>

title: Leaf-based energy harvesting and storage utilizing hygroscopic iron hydrogel for continuous power generation

概念_主学科.evidence: asymmetric coating of hygroscopic iron hydrogels
a water gradient is established between areas with/without hydrogel coating
forming electrical double layers over the leaf surface
showing capacitance-like behavior for energy charging and discharging
the specific leaf cell structures with small grooves
enabled uniform carbon coatings instead of aggregations
high electrical conductivity
resulting in 49 μA/cm 2 and 497 μW/cm 3 electrical output
resulting in 49 μA/cm 2 and 497 μW/cm 3 electrical output

title: Precisely designing asymmetrical selenium-based dual-atom sites for efficient oxygen reduction

概念_主学科.evidence: dual-atom catalysts (DACs) with well-defined active sites are attracting increasing attention
asymmetric selenium-based dual-atom catalysts that comprise heteronuclear SeN<sub>2</sub>-MN<sub>2</sub> (M = Fe, Mn, Co, Ni, Cu, Mo, etc.) active sites
heteronuclear SeN<sub>2</sub>-MN<sub>2</sub> (M = Fe, Mn, Co, Ni, Cu, Mo, etc.) active sites for the efficient oxygen reduction reaction (ORR)
the SeFe dual-atom sites facilitate a reduction in the conversion energy barrier from *O to *OH via the coadsorption of *O intermediates
selenium-iron dual-atom catalysts achieves superior alkaline ORR performance, with a half-wave potential of 0.926 V vs. a reversible hydrogen electrode
the SeN<sub>2</sub>-FeN<sub>2</sub>-based Zn-air battery has a high specific capacity (764.8 mAh g<sup>-1</sup>) and a maximum power density (287.2 mW cm<sup>-2</sup>)
the SeN<sub>2</sub>-FeN<sub>2</sub>-based Zn-air battery has a high specific capacity (764.8 mAh g<sup>-1</sup>)
a maximum power density (287.2 mW cm<sup>-2</sup>)
This work may provide a good perspective for designing heteronuclear DACs to improve ORR efficiency
compared with the Se or Fe single-atom sites, the SeFe dual-atom sites facilitate a reduction in the conversion energy barrier from *O to *OH
